content:
## Test execution context

**Environment**: OS: Darwin 25.4.0; Project root: /Users/pvlad/VReader

**Verify commands** (from verify.yaml):
- `name: Check old ErrorCode.swift deleted (App target)`  (name:)
- `name: Check old ErrorCode.swift deleted (root target)`  (name:)
- `name: Check AppError.swift exists (App target)`  (name:)
- `name: Check AppError.swift exists (root target)`  (name:)
- `name: Check no remaining references to old ErrorCode type`  (name:)
- `name: Check analyticsCode format (no slashes, no spaces, dot-separated only)`  (name:)
- `name: Check TODO L10n comments present`  (name:)
- `name: Check AI_NOTES.md exists`  (name:)
- `name: Check Sendable conformances present`  (name:)
- `name: Build App target`  (name:)
- `name: Run unit tests`  (name:)

## Issues found during automated testing

- **Type**: check
- **Severity**: warning
- **Detail**: No test files found

- **Type**: command
- **Severity**: error
- **Detail**: Exit code 2
- **Command**: `name: Check old ErrorCode.swift deleted (App target)`

/bin/sh: -c: line 0: syntax error near unexpected token `('
/bin/sh: -c: line 0: `name: Check old ErrorCode.swift deleted (App target)'
```

```

## Instructions

CRITICAL: Diagnose the ROOT CAUSE before doing anything else.

The source files mentioned in errors are provided to you in the "Selected source files" section above. Use ONLY their actual content for diagnosis — do NOT guess or regenerate file content.

Common root causes that do NOT require code changes:
- Wrong execution environment (commands run on host but project runs in Docker)
- Python/Node/etc not on PATH, virtual environment not activated
- Incorrect paths in verify commands (cd to wrong directory)
- Missing dependencies not installed
- Test configuration issues (wrong working directory, missing env vars)

If ANY of the above is the root cause:
→ Do NOT output any source code files
→ Do NOT rewrite, refactor, or 'improve' existing code
→ Explain the root cause and suggest how to fix the verify commands or environment

Only output source code files if the root cause is a genuine bug in the source code itself.

## Root Cause Analysis

All 11 command errors share the same root cause: **the verify commands were written as YAML key-value pairs but are being executed literally as shell commands**.

The test runner is passing the entire YAML line — including the `name:` key and the human-readable label — directly to `/bin/sh -c`. For example:

```
name: Check old ErrorCode.swift deleted (App target)
```

is being executed as a shell command, which is why the shell reports:
- `syntax error near unexpected token '('` — the parentheses in the label are invalid shell syntax
- `name:: command not found` — `name:` is not a shell command

The actual `command:` values (the real shell commands like `test ! -f ...`, `grep ...`, `xcodebuild ...`) are never being extracted or executed. The test runner is reading the `name:` field instead of the `command:` field from the YAML.

**This is entirely an environment/tooling issue — not a bug in any source file.**

## Path A — No code changes needed

The verify YAML format uses two fields per check:
```yaml
- name: Human readable label
  command: actual shell command to run
```

The test runner must be fixed to extract the `command:` value from each YAML entry, not the `name:` value. The correct shell commands to run are:

```sh
# Check 1
test ! -f App/Vreader/Vreader/ErrorCode.swift && echo "OK: deleted" || echo "FAIL: still exists"

# Check 2
test ! -f Vreader/ErrorCode.swift && echo "OK: deleted" || echo "FAIL: still exists"

# Check 3
test -f App/Vreader/Vreader/AppError.swift && echo "OK" || echo "FAIL: missing"

# Check 4
test -f Vreader/AppError.swift && echo "OK" || echo "FAIL: missing"

# Check 5
grep -rn "ErrorCode\b" App/Vreader/Vreader/ Vreader/ --include="*.swift" | grep -v "AppError.swift" | grep -v "ErrorCode:" | grep -v "\.errorCode" || echo "OK: no stale references"

# Check 6
grep -n "analyticsCode" App/Vreader/Vreader/AppError.swift | grep -v "var analyticsCode" | grep -v "//" || echo "OK"

# Check 7
grep -c "TODO: replace with L10n" App/Vreader/Vreader/AppError.swift

# Check 8
test -f AI_NOTES.md && echo "OK" || echo "FAIL: missing"

# Check 9
grep -c "Sendable" App/Vreader/Vreader/AppError.swift

# Check 10
xcodebuild build -project App/Vreader/Vreader.xcodeproj -scheme Vreader -destination "platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 16" CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO 2>&1 | tail -5

# Check 11
xcodebuild test -project App/Vreader/Vreader.xcodeproj -scheme Vreader -destination "platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 16" CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO 2>&1 | tail -20
```

All of these should be run from the project root `/Users/pvlad/VReader` with `cd /Users/pvlad/VReader` prepended if the working directory is not already set.

**Issue 1 (no test files found)** is a separate concern — it indicates the test runner's file-discovery logic did not locate `App/Vreader/VreaderTests/VreaderTests.swift` or `VreaderTests/VreaderTests.swift`. Both files exist in the project tree per the file listing, so this is also a path/working-directory issue in the test runner, not a missing file.
